description:
Analyze the BRD4-MZ1-VHL ternary complex (PDB: 5T35) to quantify the induced protein-protein neo-interface and the target-ligand interface.

1. Fetch the crystal structure '5T35' from the Protein Data Bank into PyMOL.
2. The asymmetric unit contains two copies of the ternary complex (8 total protein chains). Focus your analysis ONLY on the first complex: VHL (chain A), Elongin C (chain B), Elongin B (chain C), BRD4 BD2 (chain D), and its associated PROTAC ligand (residue name: MZ1).
3. Compute the size of the neo-interface: Determine the EXACT number of UNIQUE BRD4 protein residues (in chain D) that have at least one atom within 4.0 Angstroms of any atom in VHL (chain A).
4. Compute the target-ligand interface: Determine the EXACT number of UNIQUE BRD4 protein residues (in chain D) that have at least one atom within 4.0 Angstroms of the PROTAC ligand (MZ1).
5. Render a publication-quality PNG figure highlighting this ternary interaction. The figure should show BRD4 and VHL as distinct cartoon representations, and the bridging PROTAC molecule as sticks. Save the figure to '~/PyMOL_Data/images/protac_complex.png'.
6. Write a plain-text report summarizing these findings to '~/PyMOL_Data/protac_report.txt'. The report MUST contain the 3-letter residue name of the PROTAC ligand, the exact integer count of BRD4 neo-interface residues, and the exact integer count of BRD4 ligand-interface residues.
